div.h1: GPU Memory Access Analysis: Coalescing Patterns
div.h2: Memory Transaction Optimization for SIMT Architectures (Compute Capability 8.x+)

ul.li: Transaction Size: L1/L2 requests are typically serviced in 32-byte or 128-byte segments.
Warp Level: All 32 threads in a warp provide addresses to the MQA (Memory Query Arbiter).
Coalescer Unit: Hardware logic checks if addresses fall within the same aligned segment.
Divergence Cost: Non-contiguous access triggers serialized transactions, increasing latency by 4x-32x.

g.text: CASE B: STRIDED ACCESS (Sub-optimal Stride = N)
